description: Grocery and settings enhancements: (1) 'Shop on Instacart' button. (2) Cross-off items while shopping. (3) General grocery list feeding into AI combine pipeline. (4) External API for general grocery items. (5) AI text parsing for bulk ingredient entry. (6) Settings page with meal type config, week start day, and household size.

userStories.title: Create Instacart edge function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Supabase edge function that proxies requests to the Instacart Create Recipe Page API so that the API key stays server-side.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge function exists at supabase/functions/instacart-recipe/index.ts
Follows the same CORS, error handling, and response patterns as combine-ingredients/index.ts
Accepts POST with body { title: string, items: { name, displayName, totalQuantity?, unit? }[] }
Transforms items to Instacart format: { title, ingredients: [{ name, display_text, measurements: [{ quantity, unit }] }] }
Items without totalQuantity or unit get empty measurements array
Calls POST https://connect.instacart.com/idp/v1/products/recipe with Bearer INSTACART_API_KEY auth
Returns { success: true, products_link_url: '...' } on success
When INSTACART_API_KEY is not configured, returns { success: true, skipped: true, message: '...', products_link_url: 'https://www.instacart.com' }
Returns { success: false, error: '...' } with status 400 for empty/missing items
Returns { success: false, error: '...' } with status 500 for Instacart API errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use combine-ingredients/index.ts as the template — copy its exact CORS headers, OPTIONS handling, and try-catch pattern. The edge function is simple: no Supabase client needed, no AI, just an HTTP proxy. Key env var: INSTACART_API_KEY via Deno.env.get(). Instacart API docs: POST https://connect.instacart.com/idp/v1/products/recipe with Authorization: Bearer <key>. Response shape: { products_link_url: string }.

userStories.title: Create frontend instacart lib module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a frontend lib module that transforms SmartGroceryItem[] for Instacart and invokes the edge function so that the UI component stays simple.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/lib/instacart.ts
Exports transformForInstacart(items: SmartGroceryItem[]): InstacartItem[] — strips category and sourceRecipes, keeps name, displayName, totalQuantity, unit
Exports sendToInstacart(items: SmartGroceryItem[], title: string): Promise<string> — calls supabase.functions.invoke('instacart-recipe'), returns the products_link_url
Throws on edge function error (error field set)
Throws on data.success === false with the error message from data.error
Returns URL when data.skipped is true (dev mode fallback)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as smartCombineIngredients() in src/lib/groceryList.ts for edge function invocation: supabase.functions.invoke('instacart-recipe', { body: { title, items } }). The SmartGroceryItem type is in src/types/index.ts (lines 125-132). Create tests at tests/unit/lib/instacart.test.ts — mock supabase client the same way as tests/unit/lib/groceryList.test.ts or tests/unit/lib/groceryCache.test.ts.

userStories.title: Add Instacart button to GroceryExportMenu
userStories.description: As a user viewing my grocery list, I want an 'Instacart' button alongside the Copy and CSV buttons so that I can send my ingredients to Instacart for shopping.
acceptanceCriteria: GroceryExportMenu renders a third button labeled 'Instacart' with a ShoppingCart icon from lucide-react
Clicking the button calls sendToInstacart with the items and eventName props
On success, opens the returned URL in a new tab via window.open(url, '_blank')
Shows a toast error 'Failed to send to Instacart. Please try again.' on failure
Button shows a Loader2 spinner while the request is in flight
Button is disabled while loading
Button is disabled when items array is empty
Existing Copy and CSV buttons continue to work unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/recipes/GroceryExportMenu.tsx. Current file is small (45 lines) with two buttons in a flex gap-1 div. Add useState for isInstacartLoading. Import ShoppingCart and Loader2 from lucide-react, sendToInstacart from @/lib/instacart, and add the async handleInstacart handler. Update tests at tests/unit/components/recipes/GroceryExportMenu.test.tsx — add mock for @/lib/instacart module (same pattern as the groceryList mock already there). Need to test: renders button, success opens new tab (mock window.open), failure shows toast, loading state disables button, empty items disables button. Also update tests/unit/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.test.tsx to add a mock for @/lib/instacart so it doesn't break when GroceryExportMenu imports it.

userStories.title: Add checked_items column to grocery cache table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a checked_items column on the combined_grocery_items table so that crossed-off items persist across page refreshes.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration adds a checked_items JSONB column (default '[]') to the combined_grocery_items table
Column stores an array of item name strings representing crossed-off items
Migration applied successfully via Supabase MCP apply_migration tool
Existing RLS policies already cover this column (no new policies needed since they use row-level auth.uid() = user_id)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create migration file at supabase/migrations/20260228000001_add_checked_items_to_grocery_cache.sql. Use ALTER TABLE combined_grocery_items ADD COLUMN checked_items JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb. The existing RLS policies on this table already allow select/insert/update/delete for auth.uid() = user_id, so the new column is automatically covered. Apply using the Supabase MCP tool: mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__apply_migration. Project ID can be found via mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__list_projects.

userStories.title: Create grocery check persistence lib
userStories.description: As a developer, I want functions to load and save crossed-off item state so that the UI components can toggle items without managing database calls directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Functions added to src/lib/groceryCache.ts (extend existing file, not a new file)
Exports loadCheckedItems(contextType, contextId, userId): Promise<Set<string>> — reads checked_items from combined_grocery_items row, returns as Set
Exports saveCheckedItems(contextType, contextId, userId, checkedItems: Set<string>): Promise<void> — updates the checked_items column
loadCheckedItems returns empty Set when no cache row exists
saveCheckedItems creates or updates the row (upsert on context_type, context_id, user_id)
Both functions handle errors gracefully with console.error (same pattern as existing cache functions)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add to the existing src/lib/groceryCache.ts file — follow the exact same patterns as loadGroceryCache() and saveGroceryCache() already there. The checked_items column stores a JSONB array of strings (item names). Use the existing GroceryCacheContextType type. Tests go in tests/unit/lib/groceryCache.test.ts — extend existing test file with new describe blocks for loadCheckedItems and saveCheckedItems. Mock supabase the same way as the existing tests in that file.

userStories.title: Add cross-off UI to grocery items
userStories.description: As a user shopping with my grocery list, I want to tap/click items to cross them off so that I can track what I've already picked up.
acceptanceCriteria: Each GroceryItemRow shows a checkbox (or clickable area) to toggle crossed-off state
Crossed-off items show with line-through text styling and reduced opacity
Crossed-off items remain visible in the list (not hidden or removed)
Crossing off an item calls saveCheckedItems to persist the state
Checked state loads from database on page load via loadCheckedItems
Checked state survives page refresh
Crossing off does not affect the Instacart export — all items (checked and unchecked) are sent to Instacart
Crossing off does not affect Copy/CSV export — all items are included
Crossed-off state resets when the grocery cache is invalidated (recipes change)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: State management: MealPlanPage.tsx manages checkedItems as Set<string> state. Load via loadCheckedItems() alongside grocery cache loading (around line 314-334 where loadGroceryCache is called). Pass checkedItems and an onToggleChecked callback down through GroceryListSection to GroceryItemRow. In GroceryItemRow.tsx: add a Checkbox from @/components/ui/checkbox (shadcn) or a simple clickable div before the item text. When checked, apply 'line-through opacity-50' classes to the text span. The onToggleChecked handler in MealPlanPage should toggle the item in the Set, update state, and call saveCheckedItems(). When deleteGroceryCache is called (recipe changes), also clear checkedItems state. GroceryListSection props need: checkedItems?: Set<string>, onToggleChecked?: (itemName: string) => void. GroceryItemRow props need: isChecked?: boolean, onToggleChecked?: () => void. For tests: update GroceryItemRow tests to verify checkbox renders, strikethrough styling on checked items, and toggle callback. Update GroceryListSection tests to pass checkedItems prop.

userStories.title: Create general_grocery_items database table
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a database table to store freeform grocery items (non-recipe items) per user per week so that users can track things they need beyond meal ingredients.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates general_grocery_items table with columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (UUID FK to auth.users), context_type (TEXT, check constraint for 'meal_plan' or 'event'), context_id (TEXT, week_start date or event_id), name (TEXT, item name), quantity (TEXT, nullable — raw user input like '2' or '1/2'), unit (TEXT, nullable), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ default now())
UNIQUE constraint on (user_id, context_type, context_id, name) to prevent duplicate items within the same week
RLS enabled with policies: users can select/insert/update/delete their own rows (auth.uid() = user_id)
Additional RLS policy: service role can insert/update/delete any row (needed for the external API edge function)
Migration applied successfully via Supabase MCP apply_migration tool
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create migration at supabase/migrations/20260228000002_create_general_grocery_items.sql. Follow the same RLS pattern as user_pantry_items (20260207000000_create_user_pantry.sql) and combined_grocery_items. The context_type/context_id pattern matches the existing grocery cache table. quantity is TEXT (not NUMERIC) because users type freeform values like '1/2' or 'a few'. The service role automatically bypasses RLS so no extra policy is needed for it — just document this. Apply via Supabase MCP tool.

userStories.title: Create general grocery items lib module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CRUD functions for general grocery items and a converter to RawIngredientInput format so that general items feed into the existing AI combine pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/lib/generalGrocery.ts
Exports loadGeneralItems(contextType, contextId, userId): Promise<GeneralGroceryItem[]> — fetches from general_grocery_items table, ordered by created_at
Exports addGeneralItem(contextType, contextId, userId, item: { name, quantity?, unit? }): Promise<void> — inserts new row
Exports removeGeneralItem(itemId: string): Promise<void> — deletes by id
Exports updateGeneralItem(itemId: string, updates: { name?, quantity?, unit? }): Promise<void> — updates fields

Exports toRawIngredients(items: GeneralGroceryItem[]): RawIngredientInput[] — converts general items to the same format used by combine-ingredients edge function, with recipeName set to 'General' and category set to 'other'
All functions handle errors gracefully with console.error
GeneralGroceryItem type defined in src/types/index.ts: { id: string, userId: string, contextType: string, contextId: string, name: string, quantity?: string, unit?: string, createdAt?: string }
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Follow the same Supabase query patterns as src/lib/groceryCache.ts. The key function is toRawIngredients() — it converts general items to RawIngredientInput format (the same shape fed to the combine-ingredients edge function). RawIngredientInput is: { name: string, quantity: string | null, unit: string | null, category: string, recipeName: string }. Set recipeName to 'General' and category to 'other'. This means general items get processed by the SAME AI that handles recipe ingredients — they'll be deduplicated, pluralized, and merged with recipe items automatically. If a user adds 'olive oil' to their general list and it's also in a recipe, the AI will merge them into one entry with sourceRecipes: ['Recipe Name', 'General']. Tests at tests/unit/lib/generalGrocery.test.ts — mock supabase client.

userStories.title: Integrate general items into AI combine pipeline and grocery UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add freeform grocery items via a 'General' tab that get merged with recipe ingredients in the Combined view — fully deduplicated and AI-processed just like recipe ingredients.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'General' tab appears in GroceryListSection alongside 'Combined' and per-recipe tabs
The 'General' tab shows general items with add/edit/remove controls
An 'Add item' input at the bottom of the General tab lets users type item name (and optionally quantity/unit) and press Enter to add
Each general item has edit and delete buttons (same pencil/trash pattern as GroceryItemRow)
When general items exist, they are included in the rawIngredients array sent to the combine-ingredients edge function (via toRawIngredients()) alongside recipe ingredients
The 'Combined' tab shows the fully merged list — recipe ingredients and general items deduplicated and combined by the AI
If 'olive oil' exists in both a recipe and the general list, the Combined view shows one merged entry
General items appear with a 'General' source recipe badge in the Combined view
Adding or removing a general item invalidates the grocery cache (triggers re-combine)
General items are included in CSV, clipboard, and Instacart exports via the Combined list
General items can be crossed off using the same checkbox mechanism from US-006
Empty state on General tab shows 'No items yet' with the add input
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
This is the most complex story — it wires everything together. Key changes:

1. MealPlanPage.tsx: Add generalItems state. Load via loadGeneralItems() alongside grocery data (in loadGroceryData or adjacent). When running smartCombineIngredients(), concat general items (via toRawIngredients()) with recipe rawIngredients before calling the edge function. When a general item is added/removed, call deleteGroceryCache() to invalidate, then re-run combine.

2. GroceryListSection.tsx: Add a 'General' tab alongside the per-recipe tabs. The General tab content is special — it's not just a display of SmartGroceryItems, it also needs add/edit/remove controls. The perRecipeItems['General'] from the AI response shows the AI-processed general items. Create a new component GeneralItemsTab (or inline in GroceryListSection) that shows the AI-combined items from perRecipeItems['General'] AND has add/edit/remove controls for the raw general_grocery_items.

3. New props on GroceryListSection: generalItems?: GeneralGroceryItem[], onAddGeneralItem?, onRemoveGeneralItem?, onUpdateGeneralItem?.

4. The add input can reuse styling from GroceryItemRow's edit mode (Input components for qty, unit, name).

5. Cache invalidation: when general items change, delete grocery cache and re-run combine. Use the same pattern as when recipes change in MealPlanPage.

For tests: mock the new props, test General tab renders, test add/remove callbacks fire.

userStories.title: Create external API edge function for general grocery items
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a REST API edge function for full CRUD on general grocery items so that external services (chatbots, Alexa skills, Zapier, other backends) can add, list, update, and remove items from the grocery list.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge function exists at supabase/functions/grocery-items-api/index.ts
Authenticates via x-api-key header validated against GROCERY_API_KEY env var
Returns 401 { success: false, error: 'Invalid or missing API key' } for invalid/missing key
When GROCERY_API_KEY is not configured, returns { success: true, skipped: true, message: '...' }
Identifies user by user_email field in request body — looks up user ID from auth.users table
Returns 404 { success: false, error: 'User not found' } for unknown email
GET support: lists items for a user/context — accepts query params user_email, context_type, context_id
POST support: adds one or more items — body { user_email, context_type, context_id, items: [{ name, quantity?, unit? }] }
PUT support: updates an item — body { item_id, name?, quantity?, unit? }
DELETE support: removes an item — body { item_id }
POST with duplicate item name (same user/context/name) returns 409 with clear error message
Returns { success: true, items: [...] } for GET, { success: true, added: N } for POST, { success: true } for PUT/DELETE
Uses Supabase service role to bypass RLS for database operations
Follows same CORS and error handling patterns as other edge functions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Use combine-ingredients/index.ts as the boilerplate template for CORS, OPTIONS, try-catch. This function DOES need a Supabase client (createClient with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) to bypass RLS and query auth.users. Auth pattern: check x-api-key header against Deno.env.get('GROCERY_API_KEY'). User lookup: query auth.users by email to get UUID, then use that for all general_grocery_items operations. The context_type is typically 'meal_plan' and context_id is a week_start date like '2026-03-02'. For GET requests, method detection via req.method. For the body-based methods, parse with req.json(). Important: this function sets verify_jwt: false when deployed (since external APIs won't have a Supabase JWT), but authenticates via the x-api-key instead. Set up env: supabase secrets set GROCERY_API_KEY=<key>. Example usage from curl:

curl -X POST https://<project>.supabase.co/functions/v1/grocery-items-api \
  -H 'x-api-key: <key>' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"user_email": "daniel@example.com", "context_type": "meal_plan", "context_id": "2026-03-02", "items": [{"name": "paper towels"}, {"name": "dish soap"}]}'

userStories.title: Create AI grocery text parser edge function
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an edge function that takes freeform text and uses AI to parse it into structured grocery items (name, quantity, unit, category) so that users can bulk-add items by pasting a note.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge function exists at supabase/functions/parse-grocery-text/index.ts
Follows the same CORS, error handling, and response patterns as combine-ingredients/index.ts
Accepts POST with body { text: string }
Uses Anthropic API (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to parse the text into individual items
Handles any input format: comma-separated, line-separated, natural language, messy notes, or mixed
Returns { success: true, items: [{ name, quantity, unit, category }] } where category is one of the 10 GroceryCategory values
AI assigns appropriate categories (produce, meat_seafood, dairy, pantry, spices, frozen, bakery, beverages, condiments, other)
Items without explicit quantities get null for quantity and unit
Handles ambiguous items gracefully — e.g., 'milk' gets category 'dairy', 'paper towels' gets 'other'
When ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not configured, returns { success: true, skipped: true, message: '...' }
Returns { success: false, error: '...' } with status 400 for empty/missing text
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use combine-ingredients/index.ts as the template for CORS, OPTIONS, Anthropic API call pattern. The AI prompt should be simpler than combine-ingredients — it just needs to parse text into items, not merge/deduplicate. Prompt should instruct: 'Parse this grocery list text into individual items. The text may be comma-separated, line-separated, or natural language. Extract name (clean product name), quantity (number or null), unit (string or null), and category (one of: produce, meat_seafood, dairy, pantry, spices, frozen, bakery, beverages, condiments, other). Return JSON array.' Use the same CATEGORY_OVERRIDES logic from src/lib/groceryList.ts as guidance in the prompt (oils → pantry, tofu → meat_seafood, etc). Use claude-haiku or claude-sonnet for speed since this is a simpler task than combine-ingredients.

userStories.title: Add bulk paste UI to General tab
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste a block of text with multiple grocery items and have the AI parse them into individual items on my General list, so I can quickly add many items at once.
acceptanceCriteria: The General tab has a 'Paste list' or 'Bulk add' button that opens a textarea input
User can paste or type freeform text (any format: comma-separated, line-separated, natural language)
A 'Parse' button sends the text to the parse-grocery-text edge function
While parsing, a loading spinner is shown
On success, the parsed items are displayed as a preview list showing name, quantity, unit, and category
User can review and remove individual parsed items before confirming
A 'Add all' or 'Confirm' button adds all parsed items to general_grocery_items via addGeneralItem()
After adding, the textarea clears and the General tab shows the new items
Adding parsed items invalidates the grocery cache (triggers re-combine with recipe ingredients)
On parse error, shows a toast error message
Duplicate items (already in the general list) are flagged or skipped during add
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add this to the General tab UI built in US-009. The bulk paste flow: textarea → parse → preview → confirm → add. The preview step is important — it lets users verify the AI parsed correctly before committing. Show each parsed item with its detected category badge. If an item already exists in the user's general list for this week, show it dimmed or with a 'duplicate' label and skip it on confirm. The textarea can be a Dialog/modal or an expandable section on the General tab. Use supabase.functions.invoke('parse-grocery-text', { body: { text } }) to call the edge function. After confirming, loop through parsed items and call addGeneralItem() for each. Then invalidate cache and re-combine. For tests: mock the edge function, test the preview renders parsed items, test confirm adds items, test duplicate handling.

userStories.title: Add bulk paste ingredients to recipe editing
userStories.description: As a user adding or editing a recipe, I want to paste a block of ingredient text and have the AI parse it into individual ingredient rows, so I don't have to enter each ingredient one by one.
acceptanceCriteria: IngredientFormRows component has a 'Paste ingredients' button alongside the existing 'Add Ingredient' button
Clicking 'Paste ingredients' opens a textarea (inline or modal) where the user can paste freeform text
A 'Parse' button sends the text to the same parse-grocery-text edge function used by US-012
While parsing, a loading spinner is shown on the Parse button
On success, the parsed items are converted to IngredientRow format and appended to the existing rows
Each parsed item populates: quantity, unit, name, and category fields — matching the existing row structure
User can review, edit, or remove parsed rows using the normal row controls before saving the recipe
Existing manually-entered rows are preserved — parsed items are appended, not replaced
On parse error, shows a toast error message
The textarea closes after successful parsing
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify src/components/recipes/IngredientFormRows.tsx (152 lines). The component currently has rows managed via onRowsChange prop and an 'Add Ingredient' button at the bottom. Add a 'Paste ingredients' button next to it (use ClipboardPaste icon from lucide-react). The parse flow is simpler than US-012 because there's no preview/confirm step — parsed items just become editable IngredientRow entries that the user can modify before saving the whole recipe. The conversion from parse result to IngredientRow: { id: crypto.randomUUID(), name: parsed.name, quantity: String(parsed.quantity) or '', unit: parsed.unit or '', category: parsed.category }. Use createBlankRow() pattern from ingredientRowTypes.ts but populate with parsed values. Reuses the same parse-grocery-text edge function from US-011 — no new backend work. Tests at tests/unit/components/recipes/IngredientFormRows.test.tsx — test paste button renders, mock edge function, test rows are appended.

userStories.title: Add meal_types and week_start_day columns to user_preferences
userStories.description: As a developer, I want new columns on the existing user_preferences table so that users can configure which meal types appear in the meal plan grid and which day their week starts on.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration adds meal_types TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{breakfast,lunch,dinner}' column to user_preferences
Migration adds week_start_day INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 column to user_preferences (0=Sunday, 1=Monday)
week_start_day has CHECK constraint: week_start_day IN (0, 1)
Migration applied successfully via Supabase MCP apply_migration tool
Existing RLS policy already covers new columns (row-level auth.uid() = user_id)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create migration at supabase/migrations/20260228000003_add_meal_settings_to_user_preferences.sql. The user_preferences table already exists (created in 20260214000002_meal_planning.sql) with columns: dietary_restrictions, cuisine_preferences, disliked_ingredients, household_size, cooking_skill, max_cook_time_minutes. It has RLS with 'Users can manage their own preferences' policy for ALL using auth.uid() = user_id. Use ALTER TABLE to add the two new columns. meal_types stores which meals the user wants in their grid (any subset of breakfast/lunch/dinner, minimum 1). week_start_day: 0=Sunday (default, current behavior), 1=Monday. Apply via Supabase MCP tool.

userStories.title: Create settings page with preferences lib
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Settings page accessible from the hamburger menu where I can configure my meal planning preferences (meal types, week start day, household size).
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at src/lib/userPreferences.ts with loadUserPreferences() and saveUserPreferences() functions
loadUserPreferences returns defaults when no row exists: { mealTypes: ['breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner'], weekStartDay: 0, householdSize: 2 }
saveUserPreferences upserts on user_id (creates row if none exists, updates if exists)
File exists at src/pages/Settings.tsx
Route /settings is registered in the app router
Hamburger menu in Dashboard.tsx has a 'Settings' item (with Settings/Gear icon) that navigates to /settings
Settings page shows three sections: Meal Types, Week Start Day, Household Size
Meal Types section has checkboxes for Breakfast, Lunch, and Dinner
At least one meal type must remain checked — disabling the last one is prevented with a toast warning
Week Start Day section has a toggle or select for Sunday vs Monday
Household Size section has a number input (minimum 1)
Changes save automatically or via a Save button with success toast
Settings page has a back button/link to return to the dashboard
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Two parts:

1. src/lib/userPreferences.ts — Follow the same Supabase query patterns as src/lib/groceryCache.ts. loadUserPreferences(userId): query user_preferences table, return { mealTypes, weekStartDay, householdSize } with defaults if no row exists. saveUserPreferences(userId, prefs): upsert on user_id. The household_size column already exists in the table.

2. src/pages/Settings.tsx — A standalone page (not a tab within Dashboard). Use the same styling patterns as other pages (container, card layout). For meal types: use Checkbox components from @/components/ui/checkbox. Validation: if user unchecks the last remaining meal type, show toast('You must keep at least one meal type selected') and re-check it. For week start day: RadioGroup or Select with two options. For household size: Input type='number' min=1. Add route in src/App.tsx (or wherever routes are defined — check the router setup). Add menu item in Dashboard.tsx DropdownMenuContent, between 'My Pantry' and 'Sign Out', with a Settings icon from lucide-react. Tests at tests/unit/pages/Settings.test.tsx and tests/unit/lib/userPreferences.test.ts.

userStories.title: Filter meal plan grid by user's meal type preferences
userStories.description: As a user, I want the meal plan grid to only show the meal types I've enabled in settings, so that if I only plan dinner, I don't see empty breakfast and lunch rows.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanGrid accepts an optional mealTypes prop (string array) — defaults to ['breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner'] if not provided
Grid only renders rows/columns for the meal types in the mealTypes prop
Mobile layout grid-cols adjusts based on number of meal types (e.g., 2 cols for 1 meal type, 3 for 2, 4 for 3)
Desktop layout grid-cols adjusts similarly (meal type label column + 7 day columns)
MealPlanPage loads user preferences via loadUserPreferences() and passes mealTypes to MealPlanGrid
AddMealDialog (if it has a meal type selector) only shows enabled meal types
Existing meal plan items for disabled meal types are preserved in the database (not deleted)
If a user re-enables a meal type, previously saved meals reappear
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Key changes:

1. MealPlanGrid.tsx: Change the hardcoded MEAL_TYPES constant to use a prop. Add mealTypes?: string[] to MealPlanGridProps. Use const activeMealTypes = mealTypes || ['breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner']. Replace all references to MEAL_TYPES with activeMealTypes. For mobile layout: grid-cols needs to be dynamic — use template literal for className: grid-cols-[56px_${'1fr_'.repeat(activeMealTypes.length).trim()}]. For desktop: grid-cols-${activeMealTypes.length + 1} (label col + day cols). Actually desktop iterates meal types as rows, so it just renders fewer rows — simpler.

2. MealPlanPage.tsx: Add state for userPreferences, load via loadUserPreferences(userId) in useEffect. Pass mealTypes={userPreferences.mealTypes} to MealPlanGrid. This preference state will also be used by US-017 for weekStartDay.

3. Check AddMealDialog — if it has a meal type dropdown, filter options to only show enabled types.

Tests: Update MealPlanGrid tests to verify fewer rows render when mealTypes prop is subset. Test default behavior (no prop = all three).

userStories.title: Apply week start day preference to meal plan
userStories.description: As a user who prefers Monday-start weeks, I want the meal plan grid and week navigation to start on Monday instead of Sunday.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanPage uses weekStartDay from user preferences (loaded in US-016) to calculate weekStart date
When weekStartDay=0 (Sunday), behavior is unchanged from current (Sun-Sat)
When weekStartDay=1 (Monday), the grid shows Mon-Sun and week navigation moves by Mon-Sun weeks
MealPlanGrid accepts optional weekStartDay prop — DAY_LABELS are reordered accordingly
Day indices in MealPlanGrid map correctly to actual calendar dates regardless of start day
WeekNavigation's formatWeekRange and isCurrentWeek respect weekStartDay
WeekNavigation accepts optional weekStartDay prop
The getWeekStart() helper in MealPlanPage adjusts based on weekStartDay
Navigating previous/next week works correctly with Monday-start
The 'Today' button in WeekNavigation navigates to the correct current week based on start day
Existing meal_plan_items (stored with dayOfWeek 0-6 where 0=Sunday) display correctly regardless of start day setting
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
This story changes how days are ordered in the display, NOT how they're stored. meal_plan_items always store dayOfWeek as 0=Sunday through 6=Saturday (matching JS Date.getDay()). The display just reorders.

Key changes:

1. MealPlanPage.tsx: Modify getWeekStart() to accept weekStartDay parameter. Currently: d.setDate(d.getDate() - d.getDay()) which subtracts to Sunday. For Monday start: d.setDate(d.getDate() - ((d.getDay() + 6) % 7)) which subtracts to Monday. Use the loaded userPreferences.weekStartDay.

2. MealPlanGrid.tsx: Add weekStartDay?: number prop. Create reordered day labels: if weekStartDay=1, DAY_LABELS becomes ['Mon','Tue','Wed','Thu','Fri','Sat','Sun'] and day indices become [1,2,3,4,5,6,0]. The dayIndex passed to getItemsForSlot must map to the actual dayOfWeek value (0-6). Create a dayOrder array: weekStartDay=0 → [0,1,2,3,4,5,6], weekStartDay=1 → [1,2,3,4,5,6,0]. Iterate dayOrder instead of DAY_LABELS.map((_, i)).

3. WeekNavigation.tsx: Add weekStartDay?: number prop. Update isCurrentWeek() to compute current week start using the same logic as getWeekStart(). Update formatWeekRange() — no change needed since it uses weekStart date directly.

Tests: Test that Monday-start reorders day labels, test getWeekStart with different weekStartDay values, test week navigation moves correctly.
